content.full_text:
March 18, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attn: Enforcement Division

Dear Sir or Ms.:

I have a couple of questions regarding the Political Reform Act of 1974. I would be very appreciative if you could provide answers to these questions.

The Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act sets forth the requirement that the office holder, candidate and committee must open an account at a bank or savings and loan, and expenditures must be made from that account.

Question #1: May an office holder, candidate or committee deposit campaign receipts in a stock brokerage account such as Merrill Lynch or Shearson, Lehman, Hutton, Inc. instead of a bank or savings and loan?

Question #2: If the answer to the above question is "no", would the penalties in Government Code §91000 or 91004 apply?

Question #3: May an office holder, candidate or committee list an expenditure to a credit card company without making a brief description for which the expenditure was made?

Example: A scheduled payment to American Express - $800.

Finally, Government Code §85806 states that campaign funds shall not be used to compensate a candidate or elected officer for the performance of political, legislative or governmental activities except for reimbursement made pursuant to section 85201, of out of pocket expenses incurred for political or governmental purposes.

Fair Political Practices Commission
March 18, 1991
Page Two

Question #4: May an office holder, candidate or committee list an expenditure for travel without stating the political, legislative or governmental purpose of the trip?

Your anticipated courtesy, assistance and cooperation in this matter is appreciated.

Very truly yours,

GARY R. ODOM
Attorney at Law

GRO/rw

March 11, 1991

Legal Commissioner
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: Form 730 - Schedule H-3

Dear Commissioner:

I spoke with Lynn Martin today and she relayed that CMIA, under their designated code, does need to fill out Schedule H-3. We would like to get written confirmation of this, along with a definition, so that we may pass this on to our Directors.

We were under the impression that income from Rental Property did not have to be disclosed by CMIA designated filers; therefore, the annual statements had this section crossed out.

Your prompt reply to this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Yours truly,

Debra Marks
Office Manager
